docket_entries.description: NOTICEOF MOTION AND MOTION ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL 853 HEARING SET FOR JUN-22-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER GRANTING PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL 853'S MOTION ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. HERE, A BROAD ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS EMPLOYED THAT ALLOWS FOR ARBITRATION OF ANY ALLEGATION THAT THE "CITY HAS VIOLATED, MISAPPLIED OR MISINTERPRETED A TERM OR CONDITION" CONTAINED IN THE MOU. PETITIONER ALLEGES THAT RESPONDENT VIOLATED THE MOU FOR FAILING TO REASSIGN THE TWO TRUCK DRIVERS AND NOT PAYING THEM THE APPROPRIATE COMPENSATION. THE TIMING OF AN EMPLOYMENT DECISION AND THE NUMBER AND IDENTITY OF THE EMPLOYEES AFFECTED BY THE DECISION IS SUBJECT TO BARGAINING. SEE FIRE FIGHTERS UNION, LOCAL 1186 V. CITY OF VALLEJO (1974) 12 CAL 3D 608. THERE IS A STRONG PUBLIC POLICY IN CALIFORNIA OF ADOPTING ARBITRATION FOR EMPLOYMENT DISPUTES. THE MOU DOES NOT EXPRESSLY EXCLUDE THE CURRENT DISPUTE FROM ARBITRATION, BUT DOES EXPLICITLY EXCLUDE OTHERS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Noticeof Motion And Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER TEAMSTERS LOCAL 853'S Motion on Petition to Compel Arbitration. Appearances: Teague P. Paterson, Esq. & Susan Garea, Esq. of Beeson, Tayer & Bodine, APC appearing for petitioner and moving party; Gina M. Roccanova, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4241 appearing for Respondent and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Compel Arbitration is Granted. Here, a broad arbitration clause is employed that allows for arbitration of any allegation that the "City has violated, misapplied or misinterpreted a term or condition" contained in the MOU. Petitioner alleges that respondent violated the MOU for failing to reassign the two truck drivers and not paying them the appropriate compensation. The timing of an employment decision and the number and identity of the employees affected by the decision is subject to bargaining. See Fire Fighters Union, Local 1186 v. City of Vallejo (1974) 12 Cal 3d 608. There is a strong public policy in California of adopting arbitration for employment disputes. The MOU does not expressly exclude the current dispute from arbitration, but does explicitly exclude others. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
